I really wanted to enjoy Rematch, but in its current state, the game feels completely unplayable. Here’s why:

Team Balance & Matchmaking

-Teammates often leave right after conceding a single goal.

-Even if you’re consistently the top scorer with the most points, you end up losing again and again because the teams are so unfairly balanced.

Bugs & Exploits

-Players are abusing bugs to gain unfair advantages.

-Some animations give opponents unstoppable moves that you simply cannot counter.

Servers & Gameplay Issues

-The servers don’t run smoothly; the ball frequently teleports.

-Aerial duels are pure coin flips, depending only on which animation gets priority instead of skill.

Conclusion
As much as I wanted this game to succeed, right now it’s a frustrating and unfair experience. Between the broken matchmaking, server problems, and bugs, Rematch feels like a total waste of money in its current form.

Despues de la actualizacion del parche numero 3 puedo recomendar gustosamente el juego. Los servidores ahora van algo mejor, no me voy a quejar que despues de todo es una empresa indie. Con el nuevo sistema de reportes de jugadores y servidores se pueden reportar a trolls y a los servidores si ha desync o demasiado lag. Los jugadores ahora pasan mas la pelota, puesto que han hecho que los regates que eran exploits de movimiento como el constante uso del modo de regate para confundir al rival no es posible ademas de que el super esfuerzo, una mecanica del juego solo te permite realizar una accion sobre la pelota antes de desactivarse, evitando cosas como el golden boost y demas exploits con el super esfuerzo. Ademas empujar la pelota tu solo la lleva mas lejos haciendo que potencialmente puedas perder el balon si no juegas en equipo. Desgraciadamente he visto que despues del cambio al superesfuerzo respecto a las voleas, aun el meta sigue siendo pegar disparos hacia arriba, aunque ya no es tan efectivo como antes si estas bien posicionado ya que ahora llegas un 10% menos de rapido a la pelota y disparas 1 metro mas flojo de altura por lo que hay otras alternativas.

Je n'ai jamais été aussi hype par un jeu que celui la, le potentiel étais dingue cependant les développeurs se foutent de la communauté. J'ai joué 90h sur les deux béta cumulée et plus de 100h sur le jeu final. Aucune différence entre les béta et le jeu ci ce n'est le fait que j'ai du le payer. Jeu full buggé, aucun système de matchmaking en ranked (je suis diamant et tombe avec des mecs silver ???), aucun système de signalement (sur un jeu compétitif ???), aucun système de pénalité en cas de troll ou leave de ranked (encore une fois sur un jeu qui se veut compétitif ????????), et sans parler du crossplay qui doit arriver depuis 2 mois. 25 mises à jours pour améliorer le menu et les systèmes de personnalisations alors qu'il y à clairement d'autres choses essentielles à revoir avant ça. Donc comme dit au début de cet avis, jeu avec un potentiel énorme mais les devs ont décidé d'étaler leur caca sur la communauté. Dommage. Après avoir laissé 2 mois aux dévs pour régler les problèmes cités ci-dessus qui paraissent obvious je pense qu'ils ne les régleront jamais et que le jeu est voué à mourir. Je regrette d'avoir dépenser autant de temps et d'argent dans le jeu. Je finirai par dire que si vous n'avez pas encore acheter le jeu et que vous hésitez ne le faite pas. (sauf si vous aimez qu'on vous étale du caca au visage)

I love this game, but it should have been released in early access.  It was clearly not a finished game on release, missing core things like a retry option for training drills instead of having to go back to the main menu to then navigate through the menus again to re-run a drill.  There are also many game breaking issues in matches as well with ball de-sync and priority issues that make interactions unpredictable and sometimes nonsensical.

That being said, when the game works, it has been the most fun I've had on a multiplayer game since The Last of Us.  Unfortunately, it has a long way to go before I would recommend purchasing.  Unless you would like to support Slo Clap in making this game as good as it can be, save your money until a few more updates come out.  I will change my review to positive once I feel the game is where it should be.

1) Хорошая оптимизация, регистрация движений, сервера работают стабильно, технических проблем почти нет, это очень радует на фоне сотни других поделок, которые еле дышат, все скрепят и пахнут ранними билдами, порой кажется работать качественно в наше время больше нонсенс, чем правило, такое себе.
2) Стильная, вроде как, сразу видно что-то в духе будущего Marathon от Bangie, не разделяю общего восторга, очень даже на любителя такого рода минимализм с буйством красок, странное, но понятное решение. Его легче подать молодой аудитории и даже в плане графической стабильности есть профит.
3) Геймплэй - 10 из 10 - что может быть здесь ещё, очень глубоко, максимально интересно, разнообразно.
Игра в нападении требует практики, вратаря - больше реакция, защита - вообще самая лучшая часть игры,
кто-то не выкусил, но в футболе большая часть работы идёт без мяча, движение, выбор позиции, выучка.
Можно ныть, а можно принять реальность, можно посравнивать ещё с классной Rocket League, там схоже,
но не так глубоко, в основном на физике построена вся движуха, здесь нет. Учитесь думать, ребята!
4) Цена, регионалка, вообще обсуждать не будем, у каждого свои запросы, но стоимость доступная...
5) Командная игра - это корень, тут есть много игроков, которые этого не понимают, но уверяю вас, в первые 2 недели их было на 90% больше, чем сейчас, да и формирование собственной команды никто не

Rematch is the closest thing to playing the actual sport of soccer. You control an individual player on small team (3v3,4v4,5v5) in an indoor soccer match. The gameplay is addicting. You're trying to find ways pass, shoot, skill your way around the opposing team. You'll celebrate the moments when you make that last minute save or score an overtime volley. The art style is good and performance seems to be solid.

The playerbase is mostly friendly, but a toxic player can ruin a game quickly. The battle pass progresses agonizing slow. There is also a limited amount of modes and no real single player options. There are even minor frustrations like no replay option for training modes. 

[Edit]: The recent updates have improved the netcode and feel of the game. However the possession-based mechanics will occasionally lead to glitchy moments. Crossplay has improved search times dramatically.

With the recent updates I'm comfortable recommending the game as long as you're mainly looking for an online multiplayer experience.

Game launched 2 months ago with the bare game mechanics in place - tight, great 3v3, 4v4 or 5v5 gameplay. To be clear I love the game. However, I cannot recommend right now for a few reasons:

1. In the 2 months since launch, servers have become very bad, even though the game has bled a lot of its players. Being such a fast-paced game, having random spikes of lag every 30 seconds is not acceptable. The ingame overlay shows sub-30 ping and no packet loss, so I'm not sure what this is. One moment you caught the ball, and the next moment it's in your goal.

2. Devs seem to basically be sleeping on the job. They say they are working, but in the 2 months since launch they've only had maybe a couple very small, minor patches that removed a few crazy exploits, but nothing much else worth shouting about. The game shipped WITHOUT a reporting system, so cheaters and griefers have been roaming free with absolutely no way to report them in-game. Devs posted 1 month after launch that they plan to add a reporting system, but only 6-9 weeks after. You can't convince me this wasn't an oversight. Also, they are not that small a team (80 pax?). The only thing that regularly updates is their b-grade cosmetics store.
 
3. Dev communication is pretty poor and irregular. I'm not sure if they even have a community manager. I've tried numerous times tagging their account on Reddit to flag out glitches or issues in the past, but they don't seem to use that account much.

4. There are still some ridiculous, literally uncounterable moves that are left unaddressed, possibly never to be removed. Some people will say they are "skill moves", but they are physics-defying moves that is unfun to play against.

5. Ranked mode is a bit of a joke. Griefers or quitters can leave the game with hardly any penalty, and the rest of the team for some reason can sometimes still lose their ranked points when losing 4v5. Again, not fixed after 2 months since launch.

Rematch has a great core — the movement feels fluid, has a nice gait, and carries an energy you won’t find in other soccer titles. However, for me personally, there are a lot of negatives that made it a worse experience the deeper I went — which is why I can’t recommend this title.

The netcode is really bad — and somehow only gets worse with each update. While the devs have been fixing other things, the core issue remains. It’s hard to tell whether any patch is actually positive or negative when the online connection itself decides if your interactions even work properly.

The shooting animation seems to have absurd priority over shorter ones like passing. Your player having no collision unless you’re holding a defensive stance feels awkward — especially when the ball just phases through you. I get that they wanted to add a skill element to interceptions, but good positioning should already count for that. Sometimes the ball even seems indecisive about who has possession, leading to random teleports after tackles.

The playerbase can be incredibly toxic for no reason — and players will throw matches at the drop of a hat. Having no surrender option, no text chat or report/mute system, and only four unchangeable pings makes this problem even worse.

After a few major patches and the launch of Season 1, not much has changed. Most of the issues and bugs from the beta are still there — and the netcode has somehow gotten worse. It’s genuinely disappointing, because I really enjoyed the game for a while. Taking the time to fix the netcode alone could’ve alleviated so many of these problems. Such a shame. 4/10.

Good soccer/football game. Could use a fair amount of polish, but I have faith that the devs care.

It feels like you are a kid again playing football with your mates. The ball can bounce off the wall, the referee and fouls are non-existent and you lose the track of time until your mom calls you back home at dusk. 

The devs captured that childhood feeling when the game was just pure limitless fun yet managed to create a fairly accurate simulator of football itself. Positioning matters, vision matter, football sense matters. And the way you press buttons also matters. 

They nailed the match length perfectly too (6 mins), you always have this "one more game" feeling after a match ends. The overtime "golden goal" rule also adds intesitiy and a bit of sweat. 

The presentaion is good with a distinct art-style and janky-like, kung-fu style moves that don't feel out of place yet add to the unique style of the game. 

Let's hope cross-play ia added soon and minor glitches and weird interactions get fixed. Enjoying every minute with the game and ...  just "one more game" before going to bed!

EDIT: I really didn't want to change my review, but Sloclap has promised a lot and said they've fixed things in the patch notes, yet the game is still the same. It's just a let down every time, and I've become too frustrated with the game to play it anymore. Can they stop lying to their customers and actually fix their servers and eliminate exploits?

I only recommend this game because it's got a foundation of fun and the game isn't full price. If you want a unique experience with friends, pick it up! If you are getting it to try super hard then I think you might be disappointed. There's a slew of glaring issues with this game, and I honestly think they should have went the Early Access route instead. They just need to fix the servers, fine tune some of the mechanics, and add a replay button to the practice range.

Ok I am rewriting my review every so often as the game's development progresses. I do want to preface with that i do truly love this game and see the great potential it can be... that being said it's like a toxic ex situation. I will continue to play this game and hope that it improves.

I want more players in the game so please pick this up!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
THE GOOD:

The great thing about this game is that it allows you to feel amazing... whenever everything comes together. Being able to make amazing passes that end in scores, threading the ball into the corner so the keeper cant save, or even saving those very shots goals is by far an awesome feeling. It beats out a lot of sports games because it allows for great animation to make you feel like youre the one making these plays.

I will say that I am in the minority, apparently, where people actually pass the ball in game. The community is mostly not as toxic as people suggest. I do usually just stick to the D-Pad comms. This is a very team heavy game but if you have common sense you should be able to make some plays.

They have made improvements to the netcode and responses from animations so the game feels smoother. If you played in the betas, you would know how bad it could get with people and the ball teleporting around. This isnt much of a thing anymore, for me at least.

Lastly they do allow for people to gather enough premium credits in the captains pass to afford the next one (if you dont use it for the other premium cosmetics in the shop)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
THE BAD:

Ranked matchmaking is a joke. My friend and I played all our rank up games together. So we had the same win/loss ratios at the end of it. He placed in Bronze II while I placed Gold III. The only reasoning I would have for this is that it takes into consideration of your last season ranked. This takes me to the second part of ranked matchmaking and why it sucks. You dont get placed with your ranked level. To my knowledge they put everyone in the same queue and pick for quicker matchmaking. But this just makes it a 50/50 of whether you will be placed with elites (highest ranks) or people in Bronze who just picked up the game. The games can get very one sided which tampers with progression. 

I do not like the decision of making the game $30 and then also having premium currency you cannot obtain by playing the game. I think this is because Helldivers 2 spoiled me in this matter and I truly believe that is the way to go about it. You can have the option to buy the currency if you dont have the time but allow the players that are dedicated to your game to be rewarded. Also it takes a little too much time to get tokens for the pass in my opinion. 

It does put a sour taste in the player base when there are experience breaking bugs in the game and they come out with another premium skin collab with a player or brand. It kind of gives a "we want your money more than you liking our game" kinda vibe.

At the end of the season 0 I had unlocked everything and was sitting on 26 extra tokens. Then Sloclap came out with an extra step to the pass that in total cost 350 tokens. It would take days of playing in order to catch that number. They stated that this was to give a reward to their most dedicated base. At the time the game was 3 months in and I had over 100 hours but I did not qualify? It did put a sour taste in my mouth and I put it down for the rest of the season.

Sometimes animations work against you in weird ways. This kind of goes hand in hand with the priority system in the game. You will be 3/4 through and animation only for it to be instantly cancelled because of someone has higher priority even though they have just started their animation. Sometimes the animation and the sound effects for tackling may even play but for some reason the ball sticks to their feet and the walk right through you. It's just the weird off times that can repeatedly get on your nerves.

This is a very small thing but, the player base is decreasing by the day, at least on PC. This has lead to finding the same people over and over when queuing for games. The crossplay for the game does help with queue times though. 

Lastly... I swear they will put up patch notes about fixes in the game and then delete some later on when they in fact were not fixed. 

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
I will also keep this as a way to track new bugs that come and go. this is both to show they add bugs but also fix them after (sometimes):
One of the newest bugs they introduced was allowing the enemy team to score off you by tackling the ball if you recently saved a goal and dont pass immediately (fixed?)

There was an glitch where, if you put on a certain cosmetics, it would make your leg invisible.

I echo the other negative reviews for this game. This game has potential to be a great multiplayer game, but in the state that it's in now, it is absolutely not worth it to buy it. I would heavily recommend waiting a long while and see where the developers take the game and if they can make it something worthwhile. But in the meantime, use your thirty bucks for something else.

Where does the game fail? It's the game's priority system. The game seems to give higher priority to striker moves instead of defensive moves. And when both players attempt to tackle the ball at the same time, the game seems to randomly give it to one of the players. Despite the fact that the animations APPEAR physics based, it's instead based on a priority system similar to a fighting game. However, the disconnect between what the game appears to be and what SHOULD happen and what's actually designed in the game is what completely breaks Rematch. 

What does this look like? You will hit the ball away from the opponent, but the ball decides to suddenly be made of cement and refuse to move away from the opponent as your opponent continues dribbling with it. Despite you VERY CLEARLY hitting the ball with your foot, and the animation being made. Or, you're the goalie in defensive stance (which means you automatically reach for the ball as it gets to you). A ball gets kicked right to you. Your character VERY CLEARLY REACHES THEIR HANDS UP AND GRABS THE BALL. However, an opponent pressed the shoot button about a foot behind you. Know what happens? The ball teleports to the shooter because, for some reason, the game decides that shooting animations have higher priority over a goalie's defense. Despite, you know, your character very clearly holding the ball in their hands.

Another spot the game fails is the fact that the ball is based on physics while your character is limited to a pool of select moves that aren’t physics based. For example, a ball will be shot on the ground towards the goalie. The goalie, to save the ball, will use the slide button forward towards the ball. The ball is going towards the goalie’s legs. ACCORDING TO THE LAWS OF PHYSICS, the ball SHOULD BE STOPPED BY THE GOALIE’S LEGS. However, the goalie’s slide move is designed to where it’s only the goalie’s hands that’s recognized to be able to stop any ball. As a result, the ball phases right through the goalie’s legs and goes straight into the goal.
All of this isn't hyperbole; it's exactly what happens in the game, and there is a multitude of clips showcasing this everywhere. And the worst part? These aren't bugs. It's simply how the game is designed. This leads to such an incredibly horrible and frustrating experience as you do the exact, correct, and only move in the game, only to be punished for no discernible reason as you see the ball just phase right through you.

I can go on and on about how horrible this game is designed at the moment, and also describe how bare bones it is and it should have been in development for way longer. But it seems the negative reviews here do a good job of explaining that. I just wanted to explain the core dysfunction of the gameplay and why the game is such a frustrating experience.

Sloclap genuinely have a potentially great game here. When it works, it's some of the most fun I've had online. It gets incredibly hype, and when you play with your friends, and make incredible passing plays or score some crazy ass goal, you get hooked. However, as it stands right now, the game is lacking and broken at its core, and I genuinely believe that Sloclap can make this game great. And when they do, I'll very gladly change this review to positive. But, for now, save your money and wait to see how the game develops.

The first question I had after playing this game was, "why has no one thought of this before?" The mechanics make a lot of sense and feel great. When you play with friends or get a like-minded team it's a great feeling and the game really shines. This incredibly solid core is let down a bit by some dodgy rollback which collapses the waveform on 50-50 balls leading to some surprising results, and there are a few bugs and odd meta moves here and there. The small dev team has been chipping away at these problems and I'm excited about where the game is heading and the crossplay has been a boon.

They have recently added a new mode for limited time called Fireball, and honestly, I hope they continue to add events like these that feel like skill drill games that you would play during training on real teams. They're really fun and help hone your skills/mechanics.

If you're interested in a high skill ceiling sports game, especially with friends, this is a strong recommend even before they finish ironing it out.

REMATCH is a good competitive game and has a good chance of becoming a global hit if the developers listen to players and focus on the advice of top players. It also has a good chance of becoming a game for professionals and competing in global tournaments. Unfortunately, it has a lot of bugs and numerous glitches, as well as a relatively unengaged team (according to many top players).

Although the gameplay mechanics are simple and very intuitive, and the sense of direction is very enjoyable, it allows for a certain elitism among players who dedicate their time to mastering the game.

Unfortunately, after a major BOOM, it seems to be dwindling in player numbers and with few changes encouraging return play. It's difficult to predict its future development.

All of these leaves a big question mark over the game's future.




REMATCH jest dobrą grą kompetytywną i ma sporą szansę na stanie się światowym hitem, jeśli tylko twórcy posłuchają graczy i skupią się na radach czołowych graczy. Ma też duże szanse na stanie się grą dla zawodowców i na światowe turnieje. Niestety posiada sporo błędów oraz liczne glicze, jak i mało zaangażowany (zdaniem wielu czołowych graczy) zespół.




Mimo iż mechaniki rozgrywki są proste i bardzo intuicyjne w swojej konstrukcji, a wyczucie kierunku bardzo przyjemne. Pozwala ona na swojego rodzaju elitarność grupy graczy, którzy poświęcą swój czas na mistrzowskie jej poznanie.




Niestety po dużym BUM i wydaje się malejącej liczbie graczy oraz małą ilością zmian zachęcających do powrotu do gry. Ciężko stwierdzić jak będzie wyglądać jej dalszy rozwój.

Mimo to oceniam grę pozytywnie z sporym kredytem zaufania. 


Wszystko to przekłada się na wielki znak zapytania co do przyszłości tej gry.

A mi el fútbol no me gusta demasiado por lo general, sin embargo, este juego ha logrado engancharme con sus mecánicas ya que se hace muy divertido y estratégico, pero ameno, lo que lo hace equilibrado tanto para quienes disfrutan de grandes estrategias y competición, como para aquellos que solo buscan relajarse un rato con partidas rápidas. Venía de jugarlo en el gamepass de Xbox y me lo he comprado aquí. No he tenido problemas de conexión, soy de España (por si eso ayuda). La única pega que le pongo al juego es que, se siente injusto que después de haber pagado, tienes contenido bloqueado y tienes que seguir pagando para desbloquearlo (cosa que no pienso hacer). Son cosméticos, pero tenemos derecho a acceder a ellos, ya que no es un juego free to play. Puedo entender que tengan problemas económicos para mantener servidores y demás, y los micropagos (por ahora, no demasiado abusivos) sean por ahora su único sustento, pero espero que en el futuro encuentren una manera de compensarlo y eliminarlos casi por completo, y que definitivamente, su modelo de negocio se oriente en otra dirección en cuanto se recuperen en lugar de ambicionar a más dinero, porque como empiecen a abusar de los micropagos y pases de temporada no le veo un futuro brillante, a pesar, de que la premisa y la ejecución si tienen un gran potencial. Cómo manejen estos asuntos va a ser el gran determinante de su futuro.
No es un juego fácil de hacer, ni fácil de mantener, es por eso que por ahora me mantengo neutral ante estas medidas, teniendo en cuenta que todavía está en desarrollo. Pero seré cauto y estaré ojo a vizor, por que no es el primer juego que me decepciona en éste sentido. 

De momento sí lo recomiendo.

First of all I love this game!

From me in the younger years being an absolute nerd playing 13h every day on my computer to only to the present playing casually on my steam deck few hours a week. 
Playing this on the steam deck to just pickup and play some intense matches and have a good laugh, perfect.
You do laugh because of all the problems with teleporting players/ball or just bad servers. The biggest laughs are when you have no idea what your teammates are doing but you have to adapt to that. 
I played football irl for 10 years when I was younger and that is a massive thing in football that you have to adapt your game depending on who you are playing with or against, it doesn’t matter how bad they are. 
Or just flick a player and make them fly the other way or rocket the ball in the top corner, beautiful.
I guess that’s why love Rematch I just wanted a game to transport back to the pitch again.

If I would treat this game like a played games when I was younger I would probably hate this game eventually. If you want only play this game competitive and must win everything then this game is not ready for that. There is to many problems with the game and that would frustrate you like hell and you probably won’t laugh at it like I do now.

I’m hopeful for this game and that they will fix all the issues that the game has and make it better, it has all the potential in the world. Everyone stay positive and if you think it’s unplayable right now come back in a year or two I would think that everything is fixed and ready to play by then.

Stay positive people, love.
